lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਤ ਲਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur raam naam kat laheeai |
lines.english: Without the Guru, how can the Lord's Name be obtained?

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਬਿਨਸੈ ਧਨੁ ਕਾ ਕੋ ਕਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: tan binasai dhan kaa ko kaheeai |
lines.english: When the body perishes, whose wealth is it?
Aasaa, First Mehl:
Without the Lord's Name, who is ours?
Night and day, center your loving attention on the Immaculate Lord. ||1||
The wealth of the Lord's Name is my Companion and Helper.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਸਮ ਕਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਉ ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸਿ ਮਿਲਾਵਣਹਾਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sukh dukh sam kar naam na chhoddau aape bakhas milaavanahaaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I look upon pleasure and pain alike; I shall not forsake the Naam, the Name of the Lord. The Lord Himself forgives me, and blends me with Himself. ||1||Pause||
The fool loves gold and women.
O Lord, he alone chants the Naam, whom You have forgiven.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਤੁ ਨ ਲਾਗਿ ਸਕੈ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: doot na laag sakai gun gaae |2|
lines.english: Death cannot touch one who sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||2||
Attached to duality, he has forgotten the Naam.
If it is pleasing to Your Will, please preserve me, O Merciful Lord.
As Gurmukh, my mind is pleased with the Lord.
The Lord, the Guru, is the Giver; the Lord, the Sustainer of the World.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਅਉਖਧੁ ਤੰਤ ਨ ਮੰਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: avar na aaukhadh tant na mantaa |
lines.english: There is no other medicine, Tantric charm or mantra.
Meditative remembrance upon the Lord, Har, Har, destroys sins.
My diseases are cured, and my pains are taken away. ||3||
The mind is diseased with doubt, superstition and duality.
You Yourself cause us to stray from the path, and forget the Naam.
Showering Your Mercy, You Yourself save us. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਭਰਮਿ ਜਪਹਿ ਜਪੁ ਦੂਜਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur bin bharam japeh jap doojaa |
lines.english: Without the Guru, it dwells in doubt, and contemplates duality.
Without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, what use is human life? ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਨ ਦੇਖਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: aad purakh gur daras na dekheh |
lines.english: The Guru reveals the Darshan, the Blessed Vision of the Primal Lord.
Beholding the Marvellous Lord, I am wonder-struck and astonished.
I have enshrined the All-pervading Lord within my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਸੁਰ ਨਰ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt sur nar sehaj samaadh |
lines.english: In each and every heart, of the angels and holy men, He dwells in celestial Samaadhi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਹੇਤੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kee bhagat het mukh naam |
lines.english: For the sake of devotional worship, we chant Your Name.
The Lord's devotees dwell in the Society of the Saints.
There is no one else equal to You. ||6||
The Messenger of Death cannot touch him with pain;
Breaking his bonds, one comes to meditate on the Lord.
The Gurmukhs are emancipated, by the Guru-given knowledge of the Lord. ||7||
O Nanak, they are liberated, through the Love of the Lord. ||8||9||
The Lord is the Lover of His devotees; He dwells with His devotees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨੁ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਲਿਵ ਜਾਗੈ ॥
lines.translit: jo jan raam naam liv jaagai |
lines.english: the Lord's humble servant remains awake to the Love of the Naam.
Aasaa, First Mehl, Ik-Tukee:
His pains are erased, and he realizes the True Word of the Shabad. ||1||
One who serves the Guru, knows his Lord and Master.
People are entangled with mother, father and the world.
Meditate on the Lord, O my friends and companions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਿ ਦੇਖਹੁ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਨੈਨੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sev dekhahu prabh nainee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Serving the True Guru, you shall behold God with your eyes. ||1||Pause||
They are entangled with sons, daughters and spouses. ||2||
They are entangled with sons, wives and others in their minds. ||3||
They are entangled with religious rituals, and religious faith, acting in ego.
The farmers are entangled by farming.
They are entangled in trade without contemplation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਡੰਨੁ ਸਹੈ ਰਾਜਾ ਮੰਗੈ ਦਾਨ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: haumai ddan sahai raajaa mangai daan |4|
lines.english: People suffer punishment in ego, and the Lord King exacts the penalty from them. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਪਵਈ ਥਾਇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: bin har bhagat na pavee thaae |6|
lines.english: Without devotion to the Lord, they do not become acceptable. ||6||
They are entangled with that wealth, amassed by bankers.
They are not satisfied by attachment to the expanse of Maya. ||5||
They are entangled with the Vedas, religious discussions and egotism.
Nanak seeks the Sanctuary of the Lord's Name.
They are entangled, and perish in attachment and corruption. ||7||
One who is saved by the True Guru, does not suffer entanglement. ||8||10||
